channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Trump Threatens Iranian Boats He Says Are Mining the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil prices hovered above $100 a barrel as reported ship seizures added to market anxiety. The White House said President Trump does not view Iran's reported seizures as a cease-fire violation.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: US seizes another Iran-linked tanker in Indian
item.description: Iran received its first revenue from tolls collected at the Strait of Hormuz. The payment was made after a recent agreement. The official did not disclose the revenue amount. US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Iran receives first revenue from Strait of Hormuz tolls, says official The Times of India.

item.title: Trump orders U.S. military to 'shoot and kill' Iranian boats mining Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Trump ordered the military to shoot and kill Iranian boats mining the Strait of Hormuz. Live updates: Trump orders U. S. military to 'shoot and kill' Iranian boats mining Strait of Hormuz NBC News.

item.title: US military board, seize another ship in international waters
item.description: US soldiers boarded and seized a ship in international waters. The Pentagon has released video of US soldiers descending from helicopters to board and seize another ship. US military board, seize another ship in international waters.

item.title: $106 Billion Loan Reflects E.U.’s View That Peace in Ukraine Is Far Away
item.description: The European Union loaned Ukraine $106 billion for military spending. Unlike previous European assistance packages, this one is heavily weighted toward military spending, meant to put Ukraine on solid footing for a long fight.

item.title: Prince Harry makes surprise visit to Ukraine in show of support during war with Russia
item.description: Prince Harry visited Ukraine to show support during the war with Russia. Prince Harry makes surprise visit to Ukraine in show of support during war with Russia New York Post.

item.title: Israel attacks kill journalist and target first responders
item.description: Israeli strikes in Lebanon killed a journalist and targeted first responders. A fragile ceasefire holds as Israeli strikes kill a journalist and target first responders in Lebanon. Israel attacks kill journalist and target first responders.

item.title: UN might keep presence in south Lebanon once UNIFIL mandate ends, says official
item.description: The United Nations may maintain a presence in south Lebanon after the UNIFIL mandate ends, according to a UN official. UN might keep presence in south Lebanon once UNIFIL mandate ends, says official The Times of Israel.

item.title: US to ‘shoot and kill’ Iranian boats laying mines in Hormuz, Trump says
item.description: US President Trump said the US will shoot and kill Iranian boats laying mines in the Hormuz Strait. US says it intercepted another tanker carrying Iranian oil as Trump underscores alleged infighting within Iran's leadership.

item.title: Indian seafarer among crew of ship held by Iran in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: An Indian seafarer is among 21 crew members held by Iran onboard the Epaminondas in the Strait of Hormuz. Of two vessels in Iranian custody, one Indian is among the 21 crew onboard Epaminondas.

item.title: Milan tech firm Bending Spoons picks banks for $20 billion US listing, sources say
item.description: Milan tech firm Bending Spoons has chosen banks for a $20 billion US listing, sources reported. Milan tech firm Bending Spoons picks banks for $20 billion US listing, sources say.

item.title: Warner Bros shareholders back US$110 billion merger with Paramount Skydance
item.description: Warner Bros shareholders have approved a $110 billion merger with Paramount and Skydance. Warner Bros shareholders back US$110 billion merger with Paramount Skydance.

item.title: A Lost Tribe in India Makes Its Exodus to Israel
item.description: Israel welcomed the B'nei Menashe, a tribe from India who claim to be the descendants of a king exiled 2,800 years ago, with some evidence supporting their claim. The group has been immigrating to Israel in recent years.

item.title: Trump orders U.S. Navy to 'shoot, kill' small boats laying mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Pakistan's minister and a US envoy discussed efforts for a second round of US-Iran talks. Iran’s vow not to reopen the Strait of Hormuz so long as a U. S. naval blockade remained in place.

item.title: Journalist 'cleared of all charges' after imprisonment for posting Iran war videos
item.description: A Kuwaiti journalist was released from custody after being detained for posting Iran war videos. The journalist had been imprisoned for an undisclosed period. A journalist who was detained in Kuwait after posting Iran war videos has been released from custody, Sky News understands.

item.title: Trump ranks Nato allies in tiers based on defence spending and support in Iran war
item.description: US President Trump ranked NATO allies in tiers based on their defence spending and support in the Iran war. The rankings were not publicly disclosed. ‘Naughty or nice?’: Trump ranks Nato allies in tiers based on defence spending and support in Iran war The Times of India.

item.title: Trump Orders US Navy To "Shoot And Kill" Boats Putting Mines In Hormuz
item.description: US President Trump ordered the US Navy to shoot and kill boats laying mines in the Hormuz Strait. Trump extended a ceasefire while maintaining an American blockade. Trump Orders US Navy To "Shoot And Kill" Boats Putting Mines In Hormuz.

item.title: The Generals Who Are Now Running Iran
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death led to a shift in Iran's leadership structure, with the Revolutionary Guards gaining more power. A collective leadership has taken hold in the country. The killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ushered in a new form of collective leadership in the country, with more power for the Revolutionary Guards.

item.title: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, leaving five critically hurt
item.description: Two trains collided in Denmark, leaving five people critically hurt, after an accident at a level crossing north of Copenhagen on Thursday. The accident happened early on Thursday at a level crossing north of Copenhagen.

item.title: Russian Officials Say Town’s Air Is Toxic, Days After Strike on Oil Refinery
item.description: Ukraine's strike on an oil refinery in Tuapse has left residents reporting dark, oily toxins falling from the sky, with Russian officials saying the town's air is toxic. Since Ukraine attacked the refinery in Tuapse, starting a huge fire, residents have reported drops of dark, oily toxins falling from the sky.
